description:
The mutator catalog (arith=AOR, compare=ROR, boolean=LCR, literal=CRP, plus Elixir-specific result_tuple/guard_drop/case_drop/with_swap/else_removal) is well-chosen but uncited. A sentence or two linking standard mutation-testing concepts (and noting which operators are Elixir-idiomatic and have no language-agnostic parallel) would bolster the efficacy story for a technical audience.

Acceptance: README or a docs page grounds the operator catalog in standard mutation-testing terminology with at least one reference.

comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED

Session: spawned impl-only worker (orchestrate-epic); session UUID not resolvable from backgrounded worktree context
Log: ~/.claude/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex-literature-grounding/<session-uuid>.jsonl

Commit: 22c29d2
Files touched:
- README.md (docs only; +44 lines)

Tests added: none — docs-only change, no behavioural surface.
Stubs flipped: none — this ticket advances no spec requirement/verification stub; mutators.spec.md already documents the catalog, this grounds it for the README audience.

Summary:
Added a new README section "## The mutator catalog and its lineage", placed
right after the existing "## Why mutagen_ex (and how it differs from muzak)"
section (added by .1) so it composes with — does not duplicate or rewrite —
that positioning content.

The section:
- Maps the four language-agnostic mutators onto the classic selective-mutation
  operator set: arith=AOR (Arithmetic Operator Replacement), compare=ROR
  (Relational Operator Replacement), boolean=LCR (Logical Connector
  Replacement), literal=CRP (Constant Replacement).
- Cites two authoritative references with DOIs: Offutt, Lee, Rothermel, Untch &
  Zapf, "An Experimental Determination of Sufficient Mutant Operators," ACM
  TOSEM 5(2), 1996 (doi:10.1145/227607.227610); and Jia & Harman's survey, IEEE
  TSE 37(5), 2011 (doi:10.1109/TSE.2010.62).
- Documents the six Elixir-idiomatic mutators (result_tuple, guard_drop,
  with_swap, else_removal, case_drop, pipeline) that have no clean
  language-agnostic parallel, with a one-line rationale each for why the
  classic catalogs don't cover them. case_drop notes its loose kinship to the
  statement/clause-deletion (SDL) family while flagging the CaseClauseError
  behaviour that makes it distinct (cross-ref to Known limitations).

Catalog cross-checked against .spec/specs/mutators.spec.md — all ten mutators
covered, operator semantics match the spec catalog verbatim.

Acceptance ("README or a docs page grounds the operator catalog in standard
mutation-testing terminology with at least one reference") satisfied: two
references, all four standard operators named, Elixir-idiomatic set
distinguished per the ticket's explicit ask.

Deferred must requirements: none.

Verification:
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors: clean (exit 0)
- mix test --seed 0: 640 tests, 0 failures (19 excluded)

VERIFY: PASS
Session: 033814be-550f-48f5-ab4a-ac141d9e94a3
Log: ~/.claude/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/033814be-550f-48f5-ab4a-ac141d9e94a3.jsonl

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 1 (README.md)
- Files outside Allowed globs: 0 (ticket declares no Allowed-touches frontmatter; acceptance is README/docs-grounding. README.md is the sole sensible target and was the only file touched.)
- Files matching Out-of-Scope globs: 0 (no Out-of-Scope frontmatter declared)

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: 5, passed: 5 (mutators.spec.md v1-v5: mutators_test, id_stability_test, mutators_property_test, --only validate, case_drop_classification_test — all green at seed 0)
- Requirements coverage: ticket Advances no spec requirement/verification stub. It is a docs-grounding deliverable; acceptance = 'README or docs page grounds the operator catalog in standard mutation-testing terminology with at least one reference.' Acceptance MET: section 'The mutator catalog and its lineage' names all four standard operators (AOR/ROR/LCR/CRP) and cites two DOIs.
- Intent-level scope concerns: none. mix.exs untouched (no new deps). Docs-only, +44 lines.
- Project verification: pass (mix compile --warnings-as-errors exit 0; mix test --seed 0 = 640 tests, 0 failures, 19 excluded)
- Audit-expanded scope: none

Source-claim tracing (step 10):
- All ten mutators named in README exist in lib/mutagen_ex/mutators/ and in .spec/specs/mutators.spec.md, with matching semantics:
  - arith: +/-, */ -> lib arith.ex confirms; README/AOR mapping correct.
  - compare: == != , < >=, > <= -> compare.ex moduledoc confirms; ROR mapping correct.
  - boolean: and/or, &&/||, drop not/! -> boolean.ex mutate clauses confirm; LCR mapping correct.
  - literal: true<->false, small ints -> literal.ex mutate clauses confirm; CRP mapping correct.
  - result_tuple: {:ok,x}<->{:error,x} -> result_tuple.ex confirms.
  - guard_drop: drops function-clause guard -> guard_drop.ex confirms.
  - with_swap: reorders with clauses -> with_swap.ex confirms.
  - else_removal: drops else of if/with -> else_removal.ex confirms.
  - case_drop: drops last case/cond clause; README's SDL-kinship + CaseClauseError note matches mutators.spec.md r8 verbatim in intent.
  - pipeline: reorders adjacent |> segments -> pipeline.ex confirms.
- Operator-name mappings (AOR/ROR/LCR/CRP): all correctly attributed to selective-mutation operator set.
- DOIs well-formed and correctly attributed: doi:10.1145/227607.227610 (ACM TOSEM prefix) -> Offutt et al. 1996 sufficient-mutant-operators paper; doi:10.1109/TSE.2010.62 (IEEE TSE prefix) -> Jia & Harman 2011 survey. Both hyperlinked to doi.org. No fabrication/mismatch detected.
- README 'see Known limitations' cross-ref resolves (README.md:407).

Verdict: PASS

Notes:
- Ticket body carries no Allowed-touches / Out-of-Scope / Advances frontmatter; treated as an unconstrained docs-grounding task per its acceptance line. Scope is self-evidently README-only and that is what was touched.
- Session UUID resolved to this verification session's jsonl under the active profile config dir (CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR=/Users/mz/.claude-personal), not the worktree-encoded path the implementer guessed at.

AUDIT: APPROVE (auto — selective audit)
Session: orchestrator-mutagen-bqd
Log: /Users/mz/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/ (orchestrate-epic manager session)

Reasoning: VERIFY reported PASS with full stub coverage (5/5), full source-claim tracing of all ten mutators and both DOIs, zero mechanical or intent-level scope violations, and passing project verification. No auditor was spawned per selective-audit policy.
APPROVED. Merged into epic/bqd (ff 0c35acc..22c29d2). Epic smoke green (640 tests). Closing per orchestrator sign-off.

title: Mutation-testing literature grounding
